label: Diffusion & score-based models

node.label: Diffusion & score-based models
node.definition: Diffusion and score-based models learn to reverse a staged corruption process, synthesizing samples by iterative denoising or integration of a learned score field.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Diffusion & score-based models is to connect Denoising diffusion & stochastic corruption, Score matching & denoising score objectives to downstream capabilities including Image generation, editing & design, Video generation & simulation synthesis, Speech synthesis, voice & music generation.
examples: substrate for Image generation, editing & design
substrate for Video generation & simulation synthesis
substrate for Speech synthesis, voice & music generation
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Denoising diffusion & stochastic corruption, Score matching & denoising score objectives.

dependencies.rationale: Diffusion & score-based models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Denoising diffusion & stochastic corruption.
Diffusion & score-based models is learned, selected, or evaluated using the objective family Score matching & denoising score objectives.
Diffusion & score-based models extends or specializes the architecture family U-Nets & encoder–decoder convnets.
Diffusion & score-based models is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Reparameterization & differentiable sampling.
